queue_signal.headline_snippet: Tamil Nadu CM orders statewide review of hazardous industries following Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak accident - ANI News

record_snapshot.title: Tiruvallur Ammonia Gas Leak
record_snapshot.location: Kannigaipair, near Periyapalayam, Tiruvallur district, Tamil Nadu
record_snapshot.accused_gap: No public accused named or arrests reported in the frozen source set.
record_snapshot.summary: Seven migrant workers died and 67 affected workers were rushed to local medical centres after an ammonia gas leak at St Peter’s Paul Seafoods Exports Private Limited near Periyapalayam in Tiruvallur district, Tamil Nadu. The Indian Express reported that the leak spread through the residential area inside the factory campus, that about 120 migrant workers — mostly women from Assam, Odisha, and Jharkhand — were staying there, that nine critically ill patients were later moved to Government Stanley Medical College Hospital in Chennai, and that the chief minister ordered a probe. A later Google News RSS item from The Hindu said Vijay formed a three-member committee to probe the ammonia leak at the seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur district.

duplicate_guard.reason: The queue item matched the existing Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak case file, so the run proceeded as an update rather than a new-case creation.
evidence: Repo search found content/cases/tiruvallur-ammonia-gas-leak.md before any source work.
The current case file already contained the 21 June 2026 incident record and related follow-up timeline entries.

supporting_quotes: Tamil Nadu CM orders statewide review of hazardous industries following Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak accident - ANI News

field_updates.summary: Seven migrant workers died and 67 affected workers were rushed to local medical centres after an ammonia gas leak at St Peter’s Paul Seafoods Exports Private Limited near Periyapalayam in Tiruvallur district, Tamil Nadu. The Indian Express reported that the leak spread through the residential area inside the factory campus, that about 120 migrant workers — mostly women from Assam, Odisha, and Jharkhand — were staying there, that nine critically ill patients were later moved to Government Stanley Medical College Hospital in Chennai, and that the chief minister ordered a probe. A later Google News RSS item from The Hindu said Vijay formed a three-member committee to probe the ammonia leak at the seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur district. A 22 June 2026 Google News RSS item from ANI News added that the Tamil Nadu chief minister ordered a statewide review of hazardous industries following the accident.
